title: Build Smarter Games with AI

modules.title: How AI Is Changing Game Development Right Now
modules.sub: Survey the AI tools already reshaping the games industry
modules.description: You map the current landscape of AI in game development, from indie tools to AAA studio pipelines. By the end you can identify which AI capabilities are already in production and which are still emerging, giving you a clear foundation for the rest of the course.

modules.title: Generative AI for Characters, Dialogue, and Storytelling
modules.sub: Use AI to write characters with voice, depth, and story
modules.description: You experiment with large language models and generative writing tools to create character backstories, branching dialogue trees, and narrative arcs. You make deliberate choices about tone and player agency, producing a short interactive story sample you can carry forward into later modules.

modules.title: Procedural Worlds: Using AI to Build Environments and Levels
modules.sub: Generate landscapes, levels, and spaces that feel alive
modules.description: You explore how procedural generation and AI-assisted design tools can produce environments, level layouts, and world assets at scale. You prototype a small environment concept using available tools and evaluate the trade-offs between AI-generated variety and hand-crafted intentionality.

modules.title: AI-Powered NPCs: Making Characters That Actually React
modules.sub: Build non-player characters that respond, adapt, and surprise
modules.description: You design NPC behavior systems that go beyond scripted responses, using AI to make characters that adapt to player choices and context. You prototype a reactive NPC interaction and test how different inputs change character output, refining your design based on what feels believable.

modules.title: Prototyping Your Game Idea with AI Tools
modules.sub: Turn a raw game concept into a testable, tangible prototype
modules.description: You apply the tools and techniques from previous modules to rapidly prototype your own game concept, combining AI-generated assets, dialogue, and environment elements. You make scope and design decisions under real constraints, producing a working or presentable prototype by the end of the module.

modules.title: Ethics and Player Trust: When AI Goes Wrong in Games
modules.sub: Examine bias, manipulation, and responsibility in AI game design
modules.description: You investigate real cases where AI in games produced harmful, biased, or manipulative outcomes and analyze what went wrong at the design level. You apply an ethical framework to your own prototype, identifying risks and deciding how to adjust your design to maintain player trust.

modules.title: The Business Side: Indie Dev, Studios, and AI Job Roles
modules.sub: Understand careers, studios, and money in the AI games world
modules.description: You explore how AI is restructuring roles inside game studios and opening new paths for independent developers, from prompt engineers to AI-assisted solo devs. You assess different career and business models and begin framing your own project in terms of audience, platform, and potential path to market.

modules.title: Ship Something: Build and Present Your AI-Enhanced Game Concept
modules.sub: Finish, polish, and pitch your playable AI game concept
modules.description: You complete and package your AI-enhanced game concept into a pitch-ready or playable format, pulling together your prototype, narrative, world design, and ethical considerations. You present your concept to peers or a simulated audience, practicing the communication skills needed to sell a game idea in a real professional context.

synopsis: AI is reshaping every layer of game design â from how worlds are built to how characters talk back to you. In this course, you'll learn how to use generative AI tools to prototype, design, and pitch interactive experiences, whether you're aiming for a career in gaming or just want to build something people actually play.
rationale: Gaming is one of the fastest-growing industries adopting generative AI, yet most young adults interested in game dev have no structured path to understand how these tools integrate into real workflows.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can use generative AI tools to design, prototype, and present a playable or pitch-ready interactive media concept.
source_signals: the science and implications of generative ai
Large language models are having their Stable Diffusion moment
